data.title: As a maintainer, I can verify trigger accuracy via promptfoo eval suite and incident replay
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Create a promptfoo eval suite that verifies the prior-art framework's bright-line triggers fire correctly and validates against recent confident-ignorance incidents.
**Why**: Prompt-based logic cannot be unit tested traditionally. The eval is the primary verification mechanism ensuring triggers are consistent and effective.
**Scope**:
- IN: Boundary scenario evals (search/no-search classification), false-positive exclusion evals (routine modifications), incident replay evals (ACLI priority, bash syntax cascading), eval directory setup
- OUT: The prompt fragment itself (Story 1), skill integrations (Story 2)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a promptfoo eval with at least 3 boundary scenarios confirms consistent search/no-search classification across independent agent runs
  ← Satisfies: "Verified by a promptfoo eval with >=3 boundary scenarios"
- When this story is complete, eval includes false-positive scenarios confirming no triggers fire on routine modifications (single-file logic, test reversions, formatting/lint)
  ← Satisfies: "Verified by eval scenarios confirming no false-positive triggers"
- When this story is complete, eval replays 3 recent confident-ignorance incidents and confirms agents find prior art or escalate with zero wrong-fix retries
  ← Satisfies: "Applied to the 3 most recent confident-ignorance incidents"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Prompt-based logic cannot be unit tested traditionally — promptfoo eval is the primary verification mechanism
- [Implementation] shared/ skill directory has no existing evals/ subdirectory. Must establish the eval directory structure and ensure run-skill-evals.sh discovers it. Verify that the eval runner handles shared/ (which is not a user-invocable skill with SKILL.md)
